⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇸🇦 The head of the investment committee of Iran’s Chamber of Commerce Samad Hassanzadeh said the world has not yet moved past oil, and warned that Saudi Arabia’s pipeline to the Red Sea, which carries around seven million barrels per day, could be halted by striking just one pumping station. He added that if Europe had its own oil, the price per barrel would reach $500. He also argued that any tolls for Hormuz should be collected in Iranian rials, not yuan, to strengthen the national currency. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 Reuters : U.S. President Trump is considering a broader cabinet shake‑up after removing Attorney General Pam Bondi this week, as he grows increasingly frustrated with the political fallout from the five‑week‑old war with Iran. The war has driven up petrol prices, dragged down Trump’s approval ratings to 36% in the latest Reuters/Ipsos poll, and intensified anxiety about the November midterm elections. Trump has expressed displeasure with Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, and some high‑profile allies are privately pushing for the removal of Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein. One White House official said “Bondi is not the last one”. A White House spokesman said Trump maintains “total confidence” in Gabbard and Lutnick. @wfwitness

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇺🇦 Ukraine’s Foreign Intelligence reports a growing collapse in Russia’s data center infrastructure. ❗️ Aging equipment is failing on a large scale, and due to sanctions and war-related costs, Russia is unable to replace it quickly or fully. Over the past 6 months, there has been a significant rise in outages across small commercial data centers and local IT systems. 🔸 Market sources estimate that around 20% of commercial data centers have already been affected. 📌 A similar issue is emerging in fiber-optic networks, many of which were installed in the early 2000s, with key “West–East” cables reaching the end of their service life in 2025. Visioner 🎯 Ukrainian air kamikaze drone attacks have paralyzed Russian oil ports: shipments have been halted for the second week 🔹 Russian oil terminals located in the Baltic Sea — Ust-Luga and Primorsk — have been unable to operate fully for the second week following a series of Ukrainian drone attacks. The port infrastructure has been damaged, forcing oil refineries to seek alternative export routes. 🔹 Specifically, since March 22, Primorsk has practically stopped receiving diesel fuel for transshipment, causing factories in Russia's European part and Siberia to lose a key export channel. They are trying to redirect part of the supply to other ports or transport it by rail, which is significantly more expensive. ➡️ According to traders' assessments, shipment volumes have sharply decreased — from 40–50 ships per week to isolated cases. tweet
